324	
325	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
326	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
327	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
328	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
329	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
330	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
331	  · intro r
332	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
333	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
334	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
335	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
336	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
337	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
338	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
339	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
340	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
341	      MvPolynomial.C r
342	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
343	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
344	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
345	  · intro _
346	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
347	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
348	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
349	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
350	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
351	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
352	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
353	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
354	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
355	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
356	      MvPolynomial.X ()
357	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
358	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
359	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
360	
361	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
362	
363	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
364	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
365	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
366	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
367	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
368	
369	Proof structure (iter-172):
370	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
371	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
372	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
373	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
374	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
375	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
376	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
377	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
378	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
379	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
380	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
381	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
382	  classical
383	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
384	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
385	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
386	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
387	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
388	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
389	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
390	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
391	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
392	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
393	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
394	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
395	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
396	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
397	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
398	    apply top_unique
399	    intro p _
400	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
401	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
402	      intro r
403	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
404	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
405	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
406	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
407	      rw [← h]
408	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
409	    · -- add case
410	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
411	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
412	    · -- mul_X case
413	      intro p₁ j hp₁
414	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
415	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
416	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
417	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
418	    v hx dv hxd
419	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
420	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
421	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
422	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
423	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
424	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
425	    apply Subtype.ext
426	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
427	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
428	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
429	    exact key
430	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
431	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
432	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
433	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
434	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
435	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
436	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
437	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
438	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
439	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
440	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
441	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
442	      have h := hai
443	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
444	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
445	      omega
446	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
447	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
448	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
449	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
450	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
451	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
452	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
453	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
454	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
455	    simp only [pow_one]
456	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
457	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
458	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
459	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
460	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
461	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
462	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
463	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
464	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
465	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
466	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
467	      subst heq_i
468	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
469	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
470	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
471	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
472	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
473	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
474	      subst heq_i
475	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
476	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
477	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
478	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
479	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
480	  intro y
481	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
482	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
483	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
484	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
485	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
486	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
487	    rw [htop]; trivial
488	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
489	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
490	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
491	  · -- mem case
492	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
493	    have hgen :=
494	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
495	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
496	    rw [hgen]
497	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
498	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
499	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
500	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
501	    rw [map_pow, hX]
502	  · -- algebraMap case
503	    intro r
504	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
505	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
506	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
507	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
508	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
509	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
510	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
511	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
512	    rw [hr₀]
513	    rfl
514	  · -- add case
515	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
516	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
517	  · -- mul case
518	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
519	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
520	
521	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
522	
523	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
524	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
525	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
526	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
527	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
528	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
529	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
530	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
531	      RingHom.id _ := by
532	  ext x
533	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
534	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
535	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
536	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
537	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
538	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
539	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
540	
541	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
542	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
543	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
544	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
545	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
546	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
547	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
548	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
549	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
550	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
551	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
552	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
553	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
554	
555	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
556	
557	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
558	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
559	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
560	
561	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
562	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
563	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
564	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
565	
566	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
567	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
568	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
569	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
570	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
571	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
572	
573	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
574	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
575	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
576	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
577	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
578	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
579	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
580	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
581	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
582	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
583	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
584	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
585	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
586	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
587	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
588	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
589	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
590	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
591	    exact hi.map _
592	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
593	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
594	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
595	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
596	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
597	
598	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
599	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
600	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
601	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
602	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
603	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
604	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
605	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
606	  Over.homMk
607	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
608	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
609	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
610	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
611	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
612	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
613	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
614	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
615	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
616	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
617	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
618	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
619	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
620	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
621	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
622	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
623	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
624	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
625	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
626	      ext r
627	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
628	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
629	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
630	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
631	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
632	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
633	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
634	
635	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
636	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
637	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
638	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
639	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
640	
641	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
642	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
643	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
644	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
645	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
646	
647	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
648	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
649	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
650	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
651	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
652	
653	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
654	
655	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
656	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
657	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
658	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
659	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
660	
661	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
662	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
663	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
664	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
665	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
666	
667	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
668	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
669	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
670	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
671	
672	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
673	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
674	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
675	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
676	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
677	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
678	
679	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
680	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
681	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
682	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
683	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
684	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
685	
686	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
687	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
688	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
689	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
690	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
691	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
692	
693	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
694	
695	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
696	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
697	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
698	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
699	
700	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
701	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
702	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
703	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
704	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
705	
706	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
707	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
708	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where

709	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
710	
711	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
712	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
713	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
714	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
715	
716	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
717	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
718	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
719	
720	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
721	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
722	element is finitely presented), bridged through
723	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
724	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
725	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
726	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
727	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
728	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
729	      inferInstance)
730	
731	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
732	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
733	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
734	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
735	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
736	
737	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
738	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
739	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
740	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
741	
742	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
743	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
744	  unfold GmRing
745	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
746	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
747	
748	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
749	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
750	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
751	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
752	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
753	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
754	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
755	  infer_instance
756	
757	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
758	
759	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
760	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
761	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
762	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
763	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
764	
765	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
766	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
767	`W = Gm`). -/
768	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
769	
770	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
771	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
772	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
773	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
774	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
775	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
776	
777	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
778	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
779	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
780	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
781	  η[Gm kbar]

783	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
784	
785	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
786	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
787	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
788	
789	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
790	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
791	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
792	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
793	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
794	
795	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
796	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
797	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
798	
799	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
800	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
801	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
802	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
803	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
804	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
805	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
806	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
807	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
808	
809	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
810	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
811	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
812	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
813	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
814	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
815	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
816	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
817	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
818	        GmRing kbar))
819	
820	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
821	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
822	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
823	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
824	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
825	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
826	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
827	
828	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
829	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
830	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
831	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
832	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
833	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.

835	**Status (iter-172):** body remains a typed `sorry`. The chart-ring iso

840	must be related to `Spec((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)` via `pullbackSpecIso`. This requires

842	and `awayι _ (X i) _` to the `Spec _ ⊗[Spec kbar] Spec _` form), deferred to iter-173.

844	Each chart morphism is a top-level named declaration — no buried sorries. -/
845	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
846	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
847	  sorry

849	/-- **Cocycle agreement for `gmScalingP1_chart`** on intersections of `(gmScalingP1_cover).f`.
850	The substantive `(0, 1)` / `(1, 0)` cross cases reduce on `D₊(X 0 · X 1)` to the ring-level
851	identity `λ·u = (1/t)·λ` in `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing` (where `t·u = 1`); the diagonal
852	`(0, 0)` / `(1, 1)` cases are trivial via `pullback.condition`.
853	
854	**Status:** typed `sorry`, gated on `gmScalingP1_chart` having a concrete construction. -/
855	lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
856	    ∀ x y : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
857	      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
858	          gmScalingP1_chart kbar x =

863	/-- **The over-structure coherence for the glued scheme map.** Asserts that the glued
864	morphism `(gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart … : (ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`
865	intertwines the structure maps to `Spec k̄`. Reduces to checking on each chart of the cover
866	(via `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext`) — on chart-`i`, both compositions land in `Spec k̄`, where
867	agreement is automatic from the way `gmScalingP1_chart i` is built (factoring through

870	**Status:** typed `sorry`, gated on `gmScalingP1_chart` having a concrete construction. -/
871	lemma gmScalingP1_over_coherence (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
872	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
873	        (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
874	        (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar) ≫
875	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
876	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom :=
877	  sorry
878	
879	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
880	
881	The morphism is the bare scheme map `(x, λ) ↦ λ·x` (Möbius scaling fixing `0` and `∞`).
882	Built via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the 2-chart cover `gmScalingP1_cover` (the
883	pullback of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` along `pullback.fst`). The chart-`i` scheme
884	morphism `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i`, the cocycle agreement
885	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar`, and the over-side coherence

888	
889	Consumed by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut: Cor 1.5 +

891	`σ_×(0, λ) = 0` is exposed by the companion `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`. -/
892	noncomputable def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
893	    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
894	  Over.homMk
895	    ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
896	      (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
897	      (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar))
898	    (gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar)

901	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
902	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.

905	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,

914	        gmScalingP1 kbar =

918	/-! ### (E) Product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m`
919	
920	These instances are exported for Lane B's consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`
921	(in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), so its previously local `haveI ... := by sorry`
922	ad-hoc scaffolds collapse to `inferInstance`. Each instance is justified as follows:
923	
924	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type — by composition with `pullback.fst`
925	  (`LocallyOfFiniteType` is `IsStableUnderComposition` and `IsStableUnderBaseChange`,
926	  with both factors LOFT).
927	* `ℙ¹` is reduced — **closed axiom-clean iter-168** via the chart-cover + `val_injective`
928	  bridge (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`).
929	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `GeometricallyIrreducible`
930	  on `Gm.hom` needs the alg-closed-base reduction, currently not bridged).
931	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced`
932	  not shipped at scheme level).
933	* `Gm.hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: see above). -/
934	
935	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type.** Decomposes as
936	`pullback.fst ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable under composition
937	and pullback (Mathlib's `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`,
938	`locallyOfFiniteType_isStableUnderBaseChange`). -/
939	instance projGm_locallyOfFiniteType (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
940	    LocallyOfFiniteType ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
941	  change LocallyOfFiniteType
942	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
943	  infer_instance
944	
945	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Closed axiom-clean iter-168 via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over
946	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`; each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))`
947	is a domain because the canonical `val`-injection into `Localization.Away (X_i)` (a
948	localization of `k̄[X_0, X_1]` at a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain) factors through
949	`Function.Injective.isDomain`. Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`). -/
950	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
951	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
952	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
953	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
954	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
955	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
956	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
957	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
958	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
959	    intro i
960	    -- (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i = Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))
961	    -- Need IsReduced of that Spec.
962	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
963	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
964	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
965	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
966	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
967	      fin_cases i <;>
968	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
969	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
970	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
971	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
972	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
973	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
974	        (algebraMap
975	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
976	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
977	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
978	          (Localization.Away
979	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
980	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
981	      intro x y h
982	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
983	    infer_instance
984	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover
985	
986	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Scaffold (Mathlib gap: the
987	direct `GeometricallyIrreducible` consequence of `IrreducibleSpace + Spec(domain over alg
988	closed)` is not bridged; the analogist's recipe would require base-change reduction via
989	`IsAlgClosed`-fixed bridges that are absent at scheme level).

992	instance gm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
993	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom := by
994	  sorry

996	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible.** Derives from the individual factors
997	via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (with `UniversallyOpen` discharged for free by smoothness
998	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
999	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI
1000	from `gm_geomIrred`) with `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`.

1003	individual GI scaffolds. -/
1004	instance projGm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
1005	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
1006	  change GeometricallyIrreducible
1007	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
1008	  exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _

1017	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`. Each chart is a domain ⟹ reduced. Both rely on bridges currently

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
64	
65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and
69	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) all ship from Lane A
70	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) as the instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
71	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, and `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`. The
72	helper resolves them all by `infer_instance`.
73	
74	The one remaining bridge — dominance of the canonical inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` — is
75	file-local because its proof depends on the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1` (still
76	a Lane A scaffold sorry through iter-167). It is exposed here as a named top-level lemma
77	`iotaGm_isDominant` so the helper carries no inline `sorry`. -/
78	
79	/-- **The canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` is dominant.** Here `ι` is the
80	"specialise the scaling action at `x = 1`" map `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1`.
81	Once Lane A ships the concrete `gmScalingP1` body (chartwise glue), this becomes the standard
82	open immersion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` (sending `λ` to `[λ : 1]`), which is dense in the
83	irreducible `ℙ¹`.
84	
85	Project-side bridge pending Lane A's concrete `gmScalingP1` body. -/
86	private lemma iotaGm_isDominant [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
87	    IsDominant (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
88	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left :=
89	  sorry
90	
91	/-- **Helper (pointed case): a morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A` killing `0 ∈ ℙ¹` is the constant `1`.**
92	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, if `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` satisfies
93	`ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]`, then `f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)`
94	(equivalently `f = toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ η[A]`).
95	
96	This is the pointed core of the `𝔾ₘ`-scaling shortcut: form `h := gmScalingP1 ≫ f`, feed it
97	to Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base
98	points `zeroPt`, `onePt`. The `W`-axis collapses by `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, leaving
99	`h = fst ≫ fV` (the relation `f(λx) = fV(x)`). Specialising at `x = 1` (via the canonical
100	inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` given by `λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ`) and using density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹` plus
101	separatedness of `A` (via `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, the same Mathlib bridge
102	`rigidity_core` uses inline), we conclude `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV)`. The basepoint
103	hypothesis then pins `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]`.
104	
105	**Status (iter-167):** body fully refactored — all five in-line product/Proj `sorry`s have
106	been eliminated. Four of them (`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced`
107	of the product, and `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) ship from Lane A
108	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
109	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`) and resolve
110	by `infer_instance` in scope. The fifth — dominance of the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map — is
111	exposed as the named top-level bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` above (a single `sorry` pending
112	Lane A's `gmScalingP1` body). The helper itself contains no `sorry`. -/
113	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
114	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
115	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
116	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
117	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
118	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
119	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
120	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
121	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
122	  have hcollapse :
123	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
124	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
125	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
126	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
127	    have hreshape :
128	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
129	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
130	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
131	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
132	      congr 1
133	      rw [← Category.assoc,
134	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
135	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
136	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
137	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
138	  have hcorhyp :
139	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
140	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
141	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
142	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
143	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
144	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
145	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
146	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
147	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
148	  have hfW :
149	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
150	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
151	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
152	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
153	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
154	  have hSndFW :
155	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
156	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
157	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
158	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
159	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
160	    congr 1
161	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
162	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
163	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
164	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
165	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
166	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
167	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
168	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
169	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
170	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
171	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
172	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
173	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
174	  have hιf :
175	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
176	    calc iotaGm ≫ f
177	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
178	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
179	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
180	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
181	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
182	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
183	          rw [Category.assoc]
184	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
185	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
186	          rw [Category.assoc]
187	  -- Separatedness of the target `A` over `Spec k̄` in `OverClass.fromOver` form.
188	  haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated
189	  haveI : IsSeparated (A.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := hAsep
190	  -- `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and the product geom-irred / LOFT / IsReduced
191	  -- instances are all auto-resolved via Lane A's exports in `Genus0BaseObjects`
192	  -- (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, `projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`,
193	  -- `projGm_isReduced`). Dominance of `ι.left` is the only file-local bridge, cited
194	  -- explicitly via `iotaGm_isDominant` (the `set`-binding `hιdef` exposes `iotaGm.left`
195	  -- definitionally as `(lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left`).
196	  haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant
197	  -- Globalise: `ι ≫ f = ι ≫ (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV))` gives, by dominance, `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ c`.
198	  have hf_eq :
199	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
200	    -- Promote the Over morphism equality from the underlying scheme equality.
201	    have hgoal :
202	        iotaGm ≫ f
203	          = iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
204	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)) := by
205	      have hreshape :
206	          iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
207	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV))
208	            = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
209	        rw [← Category.assoc,
210	          toUnit_unique (iotaGm ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (Gm kbar))]
211	      rw [hreshape, hιf]
212	    refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
213	    refine ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
214	      (X := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left) (Y := A.left)
215	      (f := f.left)
216	      (g := (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
217	        (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)).left) iotaGm.left ?_
218	    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← Over.comp_left]
219	    exact congrArg Over.Hom.left hgoal
220	  -- Pin `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]` via the basepoint hypothesis `hf0`.
221	  have hc : ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV = η[A] := by
222	    have hcomp := hf0
223	    rw [hf_eq] at hcomp
224	    rw [← Category.assoc,
225	      toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
226	        (𝟙 _),
227	      Category.id_comp] at hcomp
228	    exact hcomp
229	  rw [hf_eq, hc, ← Hom.one_def]
230	
231	/-- **A morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant.** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
232	morphism `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` from the projective line into an abelian variety
233	`A` (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme) is constant: it factors through
234	a single `k̄`-point `a₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`, i.e. `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
235	
236	The single-curve base case of Milne's Proposition 3.10. **Route resolved iter-164: the
237	𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`,
238	char-general. The proof body proceeds by translating in the group `A` to reduce to the
239	basepoint case `zeroPt ≫ f = η[A]` (handled by the helper
240	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`), then un-translating.
241	
242	See blueprint `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`
243	(Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10).
244	
245	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via the helper's residuals
246	(three product-instance Mathlib bridges + dominance of the canonical `Gm → ℙ¹` map). Lifts
247	to axiom-clean once those are discharged. -/
248	theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const
249	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
250	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
251	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
252	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) :
253	    ∃ a₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A,
254	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ a₀ := by
255	  -- Witness: `a₀ := zeroPt ≫ f` (the value of `f` at the scaling fixed point `0 ∈ ℙ¹`).
256	  refine ⟨ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f, ?_⟩
257	  -- Translate: form `f' := f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)` and apply the helper to `f'`.
258	  have hf' :
259	      (f / (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f))
260	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
261	    apply morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
262	    -- Show `zeroPt ≫ (f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)) = η[A]`.
263	    rw [GrpObj.comp_div]
264	    have hv :
265	        ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫
266	            toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
267	              (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f)
268	          = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f := by
269	      rw [← Category.assoc,
270	        toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
271	          (𝟙 _),
272	        Category.id_comp]
273	    rw [hv, div_self', Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
274	  -- Untranslate: `f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀) = 1` ⟺ `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
275	  exact div_eq_one.mp hf'
276	
277	/-- **A genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`.** Over an algebraically closed field
278	`k̄`, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` with `genus C = 0` is isomorphic — in
279	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))` — to the concrete projective line `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.
280	
281	Hartshorne's Example IV.1.3.5 (Riemann–Roch). Its formalisation is a genuine sub-build:
282	Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch for curves; this is the dominant long pole flagged by the
283	iter-164 progress-critic.
284	
285	See blueprint `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
286	(Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
287	
288	**Status (iter-166):** signature refactored to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`; body
289	remains `sorry` (RR bridge — iter-167+). -/
290	theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1
291	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
292	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
293	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
294	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0) :
295	    Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :=
296	  sorry
297	
298	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
299	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
300	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
301	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
302	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
303	
304	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
305	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
306	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
307	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
308	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
309	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
310	
311	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
312	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
313	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
314	axiom-clean. -/
315	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
316	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
317	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
318	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
319	    [IsProper C.hom]
320	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
321	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
322	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
324	    (f : C ⟶ A)
325	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
326	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
327	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
328	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
329	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
330	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
331	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
332	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
333	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
334	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
335	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
336	    exact _hf
337	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
338	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
339	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
340	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
341	    exact hpoint
342	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
343	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
344	  calc f
345	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
346	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
347	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
348	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
349	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
350	        rw [← Category.assoc,
351	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
352	
353	
354	end AlgebraicGeometry
355

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between
12	schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree
13	as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and geometrically irreducible over
14	`Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere. Independent of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C` and provable from current Mathlib alone
16	(Stacks 0BFD plus standard separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
17	
18	The historical motivation is Mumford's §4 rigidity for morphisms of group
19	schemes; the formalized statement is the minimal-hypothesis scheme-level
20	form, which the Mumford group-scheme rigidity follows from by specialization.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
23	
24	## Status
25	
26	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` — rigidity of morphisms agreeing as scheme
27	maps on a non-empty open of a reduced (geometrically) irreducible source
28	with separated target — is closed. See the "Hypothesis history" block below
29	for the discussion of why the original point-wise hypothesis was
30	strengthened to scheme-level equality on `U`.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
42	
43	/-!
44	## Hypothesis history
45	
46	### Scheme-level hypothesis (iter 003)
47	
48	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
49	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
50	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
51	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
52	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
53	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
54	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
55	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
56	
57	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
58	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
59	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
60	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
61	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
62	
63	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
64	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
65	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
66	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
67	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
68	
69	### Unused-hypothesis cleanup (iter 125)
70	
71	Eight decorative hypotheses (`{n m : ℕ}`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
72	`[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GrpObj X]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`,
73	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, and the strengthening of
74	`[IsSeparated Y.hom]` to `[IsProper Y.hom]`) were dropped per
75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
104	    (‹IsSeparated Y.hom› : IsSeparated Y.hom)
105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
109	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
122	
123	end AlgebraicGeometry
124

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity over `k̄`: morphisms `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A_{k̄}` are constant
10	
11	The keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0
12	Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex § C.2): morphisms from the
13	projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
14	group scheme `A_{k̄}` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` that hit
15	the identity at a `k̄`-rational point are the constant morphism at the
16	identity. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Chapter~II~§4.
17	
18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`.
19	
20	## Status (iter-126 scaffold)
21	
22	The named declaration `rigidity_over_kbar` is scaffolded with a single
23	`sorry` body. The body closure is gated on the shared cotangent-
24	vanishing Mathlib pile (`analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`,
25	iter-129+) per STRATEGY.md § M2.a + § M2.d-alt. The proof decomposition
26	into sub-steps C.2.b (reduction to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`),
27	C.2.c (image-dimension dichotomy), C.2.d (the cotangent-vanishing
28	keystone), and C.2.e (set-to-scheme promotion) is documented in
29	`RigidityKbar.tex` § "Proof decomposition".
30	
31	## Encoding choice (iter-126 refactor agent note)
32	
33	The directive offered two encodings of the source `ℙ¹_{k̄}`: a literal
34	`Over.mk (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom MvPolynomial.C))` (Option A) or
35	an abstract "smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄` of
36	genus 0" (Option B). The literal Option A is mathematically wrong as
37	written — `Spec(MvPolynomial σ kbar) ⟶ Spec kbar` is the affine, not
38	projective, line — and Mathlib `b80f227` has no `ProjectiveSpace n S`
39	construction packaged as a `Scheme` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` is the
40	general `Proj` of a graded ring, not specialised). The refactor agent
41	took the directive-recommended Option B: the source is a smooth proper
42	geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative dimension
43	`1` with `genus C = 0`. This is what the proof decomposition C.2.c–C.2.d
44	actually consume (the keystone uses the curve's `H¹(·, 𝒪) = 0` ↔
45	`genus = 0` characterisation, not a literal `ℙ¹` API).
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u
51	
52	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
53	
54	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
55	
56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
57	
58	/-- Rigidity for morphisms `C ⟶ A` from a genus-`0` curve over an
59	algebraically closed field `k̄` (encoding `ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the abstract
60	smooth-proper-geometrically-irreducible-of-relative-dimension-`1`-with-
61	`genus = 0` characterisation): every morphism from such a curve to a
62	smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `A` over `k̄`
63	that hits the identity at a `k̄`-rational point is the constant
64	morphism at the identity.
65	
66	This is the keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the
67	genus-0 Albanese-witness argument. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*,
68	Chapter~II~§4.
69	
70	**Status**: iter-126 scaffold — body is a single `sorry`. The closure
71	(C.2.b reduction via `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` + C.2.c image-
72	dimension dichotomy + C.2.d cotangent-vanishing keystone) is gated on
73	the shared cotangent-vanishing Mathlib pile (iter-129+). See
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`. -/
75	theorem rigidity_over_kbar
76	    [IsAlgClosed kbar] [CharZero kbar]
77	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
78	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
79	    [IsProper C.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
81	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
82	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
83	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
84	    (f : C ⟶ A)
85	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
86	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
87	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) :=
88	  sorry
89	
90	end AlgebraicGeometry
91

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry
95
